summary: Trojanized Vite impersonation with obfuscated remote-code loader in CLI
details: @uw010010/vite-tree ships an otherwise verbatim copy of Vite 5.4 (package.json forges author 'Evan You', repository vitejs/vite, homepage vite.dev) but appends an obfuscated payload to bin/vite.js. The trailing block in bin/vite.js uses a key-seeded string-array decoder (_$_4445) with String.fromCharCode(127) placeholder substitution to hide module names, hostnames, RPC methods, and eval. Every time the vite-tree CLI is invoked, the decoder resolves references to https.request/https.get, performs an HTTPS GET and JSON-RPC POST to remote hosts, XOR-decodes the response, and passes the result to eval(). It also spawns a detached child_process with {detached:true, windowsHide:true} to execute a second decoded payload out-of-band. The package name, scope, and forged maintainer/repository fields are designed to lure users who confuse it with the real vite package via npx or CLI install. The result is full remote code execution on any machine that runs the CLI, under cover of a Vite impersonation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
